title: Amazon Prime Day Puts ETFs in Focus
detail:
Amazon Prime Day Puts ETFs in Focus
Moz Farooque ACCA
Wed, June 24, 2026 at 10:44 AM PDT 1 min read
AMZN
XLY
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and a wide range of consumer-focused ETFs are back in focus as investors look to Prime Day for fresh signs of consumer strength.

Amazon shares rose about 1.5% on Wednesday as anticipation around the annual shopping event built. But the impact extends far beyond Amazon itself. The e-commerce giant is a top holding in 723 exchange-traded funds, which collectively own roughly 1.4 billion shares, making Prime Day a potentially important catalyst across the broader ETF market.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 2 Warning Sign with AMZN.

Is AMZN fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Several consumer and retail ETFs carry especially large Amazon weightings. The Global X PureCap MSCI Consumer Discretionary ETF (GXPD) allocates nearly 39% of its portfolio to Amazon, while the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) has a roughly 26% weighting. Other heavily exposed funds include RTH, ONLN, VCR and FDIS.

Prime Day has evolved into more than a retail event. Strong sales could boost sentiment around consumer spending, e-commerce demand and discretionary stocks, while disappointing results could weigh on a broad group of retail-focused ETFs.

title: US Online Spending Hits $8.3 Billion as Prime Day Starts Strong
detail:
US Online Spending Hits $8.3 Billion as Prime Day Starts Strong
Khac Phu Nguyen
Wed, June 24, 2026 at 10:42 AM PDT 1 min read
ADBE
AMZN
TGT
WMT
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Online spending across US retailers reached $8.3 billion on the first day of Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN)'s annual Prime Day sale, according to Adobe, coming in above the firm's earlier estimate of $7.9 billion. The stronger start could give investors a fresh signal that consumers are still willing to spend when retailers put enough promotions in front of them.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 2 Warning Sign with AMZN.

Is AMZN fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Adobe, which tracks visits to retail websites, now expects total online spending to reach $26.3 billion across the four-day event, up 9% from last year's Prime Day promotion held in July. The event remains a closely watched barometer for online retail demand, especially as investors track whether shoppers are still opening their wallets for discretionary and everyday purchases.

Amazon was not the only company benefiting from the shopping window. Walmart (NASDAQ:WMT) and Target (NYSE:TGT) also scheduled overlapping promotions, helping drive traffic and spending across the broader web, while Adobe said shoppers bought electronics, appliances, tools, home and garden products, with everyday essentials also showing strength.

title: Amazon Faces $90,000 Seller-Funds Scrutiny
detail:
Amazon Faces $90,000 Seller-Funds Scrutiny
Khac Phu Nguyen
Wed, June 24, 2026 at 10:39 AM PDT 1 min read
AMZN
Trade AMZN on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN) could face fresh investor scrutiny after a Bloomberg report detailed how an international black market may still be targeting the company's marketplace. The report described middlemen on apps like Telegram, WeChat, and WhatsApp offering sellers access to Amazon insiders who could allegedly help recover frozen funds, provide internal account information, or influence seller cases for a fee.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 2 Warning Sign with AMZN.

Is AMZN fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

The case centered on online merchant Jack Nekhala, whose Bed Scrunchie business was suspended in November 2024 over alleged review-policy violations. Nekhala said the suspension froze about $90,000 in funds and hit just as he had stocked 30,000 units ahead of Black Friday and Cyber Monday. He later said a woman showed him internal Amazon account records and suggested paying an Amazon contact to retrieve the money, with the contact typically charging 20%.

Amazon said bad actors may try to exploit its marketplace and that employee involvement happens only on very rare occasions. The company also said it invests heavily in fraud prevention, with dedicated teams and systems in place. Still, the report could raise new questions for investors around marketplace controls, seller trust, and operational risk as Amazon leans more on AI and outsourced marketplace functions.

title: Why Investors Are Finally Separating Quantum Computing Winners From Losers
detail:
Why Investors Are Finally Separating Quantum Computing Winners From Losers
Dr. Robert Castellano
Wed, June 24, 2026 at 7:28 AM PDT 7 min read
NVDA
MSFT
QUBT
AMZN
GOOG
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure
Quick Read

IonQ gained ground while D-Wave, Rigetti, and Quantum Computing Inc. all declined as investors began rewarding commercialization over scientific promise.

Jensen Huang warned practical quantum computing may be decades away, yet Nvidia deepened its quantum investments through CUDA-Q and hybrid computing initiatives.

Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, IBM, and Nvidia may capture more quantum value than pure-play hardware companies by profiting regardless of which architecture wins.

Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

For much of the past two years, investors treated quantum computing as a single investment theme. Whether a company pursued trapped-ion systems, superconducting qubits, quantum annealing, or photonic architectures mattered less than the fact that it was associated with quantum computing. Capital flowed broadly into the sector as investors searched for the next transformative technology platform following artificial intelligence.

Recent stock performance suggests that phase may be ending.

vchal / iStock via Getty Images

Investors are beginning to distinguish between companies demonstrating commercial progress and those that remain dependent on future technological milestones. This shift is important because it often marks the transition from speculative enthusiasm to fundamental analysis, a pattern that has repeated itself throughout technology history. The internet boom eventually separated Amazon from hundreds of failed dot-coms. Artificial intelligence ultimately produced clear winners such as Nvidia while countless AI startups disappeared. Quantum computing appears to be entering a similar phase.

Over the past six months, one company has clearly separated itself from the pack.

Quantum Computing Is No Longer One Trade

Investors often speak about quantum computing as if every company in the sector is moving in the same direction. Stock performance suggests otherwise. During the past six months, investors have become increasingly selective, rewarding companies demonstrating commercial progress while punishing those still dependent on future technological milestones. This shift is important because it suggests the market is moving beyond broad enthusiasm for quantum computing and beginning to evaluate individual companies based on execution, revenue growth, and customer adoption.

Don't wait: the analyst who called NVIDIA in 2010 just revealed his top 10 AI stocks. See the full list FREE now.

I show in Chart 1 that the four leading publicly traded pure-play quantum computing companies have produced dramatically different returns despite operating within the same industry.

title: Cerebras Sinks Double Digits After Soft Margin Forecast Raises Red Flags
detail:
Cerebras Sinks Double Digits After Soft Margin Forecast Raises Red Flags
Nauman Khan
Wed, June 24, 2026 at 7:12 AM PDT 1 min read
CBRS
AMZN
Trade AMZN on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Cerebras Systems (NASDAQ:CBRS) fell 10% as investors focused on a weaker margin outlook, even after the chipmaker disclosed a major new OpenAI partnership.

Cerebras Systems said core gross margin for the fiscal second quarter is expected to be 36% to 38%, down from 47% in the first quarter. Revenue is projected at $194 million, which would be up 88% from a year earlier.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 1 Warning Sign with CBRS.

Is CBRS fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Cerebras Systems reported a loss of 22 cents a share on revenue of $193.41 million for the latest quarter, topping Wall Street estimates. Hardware sales rose 59% to $110.6 million, while cloud and other services revenue jumped 178% to $82.8 million.

Cerebras Systems said it reached a multiyear OpenAI deal covering 750 megawatts of inference capacity over several years, with the agreement valued at more than $20 billion. The company also began a separate partnership with Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN) Web Services. For fiscal 2026, Cerebras Systems expects revenue of $855 million to $865 million.

title: Should You Buy Amazon Stock Before June 26?
detail:
Should You Buy Amazon Stock Before June 26?
Adam Spatacco, The Motley Fool
Wed, June 24, 2026 at 7:02 AM PDT 3 min read
AMZN
NVDA
Explore stocks on Coinbase
Trading disclosure

Amazon's (NASDAQ: AMZN) annual Prime Day 2026 is underway, running from June 23 to June 26. Some investors may consider purchasing Amazon stock ahead of the event's conclusion and the release of any early sales and engagement stats.

While the multiday shopping promotion always generates considerable buzz, it represents only one piece of a much larger picture for the company.

Missed Nvidia in 2009? This Rare Signal Is Flashing Again. In 2009, a "Double Down" signal flashed for a little-known chipmaker called Nvidia. For the first time in years, that same "Total Conviction" signal is flashing for a company 1/100th the size of Nvidia. Continue »

Image source: Amazon.
What is Prime Day really about?

Amazon created Prime Day to stimulate e-commerce activity during the traditionally slower summer period. By offering steep discounts and exclusive deals to Prime members, the company generates increased buying activity during a period of otherwise soft retail demand.

The annual event helps maintain momentum in Amazon's e-commerce segment by reinforcing the benefits of Prime membership -- keeping shoppers engaged with the marketplace even outside of the peak holiday season.

The real investment thesis for Amazon is AI, not e-commerce

In my eyes, the strongest reason to consider investing in Amazon stock lies in its leadership in artificial intelligence (AI), not in online shopping. Through Amazon Web Services (AWS), the company provides critical cloud infrastructure that powers AI applications for countless enterprises. Moreover, the company continues to invest heavily in generative AI tools, machine learning capabilities, robotics, and custom silicon.

These initiatives expand Amazon's total addressable market (TAM) and create new revenue opportunities across cloud services and enterprise solutions. While retail operations remain important, e-commerce is becoming a smaller portion of the overall investment narrative compared with Amazon's AI priorities.

Remember to think long-term and avoid timing the market

Attempting to time the purchase of Amazon stock around Prime Day -- or any single event -- is unproductive. Instead, smart investors should evaluate Amazon's diversified business model spanning e-commerce, cloud computing, digital advertising, logistics, entertainment, and AI.

Taken together, investors can better assess Amazon's ability to deliver sustained growth. A patient approach rooted in Amazon's long-term potential offers more reliable upside compared to chasing short-term catalysts.
